purpose: Next ingestion tasks for turning the research dashboard into a larger 2016-2026 festival evidence archive.

tasks.evidence_basis: MCST page lists annual regional festival information for 2026, 2025, 2024, 2023, 2022, 2021, 2020, 2019, 2018 and 2017.
notes: Schedules may change by local-government decision.
Keep raw files or checksums when download succeeds.
MCST page JavaScript calls file_download with pFileName={year}_festival.zip, pRealName={year}_festival.zip and pPath=PORTAL.DOCUMENT.UPLOAD.
GET probe succeeded for 2017-2026 ZIPs; byte sizes and SHA-256 hashes are recorded in data/mcst-download-probe.json.

tasks.source: MCST 2016 culture-tourism evaluation report plus local government archives
tasks.evidence_basis: The found 2016 official MCST item covers 43 culture-tourism festivals, not a nationwide regional-festival master.
blocked_by: no single public nationwide 2016 master confirmed
notes: Mark records selected_festival_only or local_backfill, never confirmed_master unless source proves nationwide coverage.

tasks.source: Public Data Portal nationwide culture festival standard data
tasks.evidence_basis: The page describes a national standard dataset maintained quarterly with 229 provider datasets and current local festival records.
notes: Treat as current snapshot, not historical panel.
Use coordinates/address to disambiguate duplicate festival names.

tasks.source: MCST regional festival information file dataset
tasks.evidence_basis: Public Data Portal describes an XLSX with 1,214 rows and fields including festival type, dates, budget, visitors, organization and legal basis.
notes: Registered 2025-04-29 and modified 2025-08-20 on the portal page.
Use as field-rich backbone where annual downloads are unavailable.

tasks.evidence_basis: The portal page states data is provided from fiscal year 2012 and includes festival name, business content, total cost, revenue, net cost, start and end date.
blocked_by: OpenAPI usage and traffic policy may require public data flow
notes: Disclosure threshold: metro 500m KRW and basic local gov 300m KRW.
Small festivals will be missing by design.

tasks.evidence_basis: The portal page states the data covers fiscal year 2015 onward and includes event operation, compensation, private event subsidy and related facility categories.
blocked_by: OpenAPI usage and traffic policy may require public data flow
notes: Mostly local-government fiscal category layer; use as budget context, not direct event cost unless event-level fields exist.

tasks.source: Local Finance 365 contract status and NaraJangteo bid notices
tasks.evidence_basis: Contract status page exposes JSON/XML link-type API metadata and real-time update policy; NaraJangteo bid notice pages expose procurement documents and attachments.
blocked_by: No canonical festival ID in contract data
automated API access may require key
notes: Candidate-match by festival keywords, agency, date window and service terms.
Store notice number, contract number, agency and attachment URL for every high match.
